title: Method, systems and apparatus for single cell analysis
abstract: The disclosed embodiments relate to method, apparatus and system for high throughput single-cell DNA sequencing with droplet microfluidic. In an exemplary embodiment, a method for analyzing nucleic acids within a cell includes the steps of: (a) flowing individual cells together with a material capable of forming a polymer or microsphere that retains nucleic acids into a carrier fluid such that droplets are formed; (b) breaking the emulsion and collecting the microsphere hydrogels in an aqueous fluid; and (c) performing combinatorial labeling on the nucleic acids contained within the microspheres/hydrogels.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method for analyzing nucleic acids within a cell comprising: flowing a plurality of individual cells together with a material capable of forming a polymer or microsphere into a carrier fluid to form a plurality of droplets in microspheres in an emulsions, wherein each cell further contains a respective nucleic acid and wherein each of the plurality of microspheres is configured to retain a respective nucleic acid; breaking the emulsion and collecting the one or more microspheres in an aqueous fluid; and performing combinatorial labeling on the nucleic acids contained within the microspheres hydrogels. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the microspheres comprise one or more of acrylamide, gelatin, agarose or a material capable of polymerizing after a droplet formation. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising incorporating an oligonucleotide into one of the plurality of the microspheres to hybridize to anucleic acid retained in therein and wherein the oligonucleotide defines a complementary sequence to the nucleic acid. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the nucleic acid associated with one of the plurality of cells is retained within a hydrogel polymer network of its respective microsphere and prevented from diffusing out of the microsphere. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the combinatorial labeling further comprises split-pool labeling of at least one nucleic acid retained within a microspheres. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising performing combinatorial labeling with an oligonucleotide ligated onto a respective nucleic acid of a cell. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein combinatorial labeling is performed with nucleic acid synthesis. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein combinatorial labeling comprises at least one barcode, and wherein the at least one barcode is incorporated into a reaction product. 9. A method for analyzing nucleic acids within a cell, comprising: (a) combining a plurality of molecular barcodes on a polymeric support with a plurality of cells in a carrier fluid to form a plurality of microsphere in an emulsion, wherein the carrier fluid defines a continuous phase fluid; (b) disrupting the polymeric support to capture a plurality of nucleic acids contained within a respective plurality of cells and then re-polymerizing the support material; (c) breaking the emulsion and collecting the microspheres in an aqueous fluid wherein each of the plurality of nucleic acids is retained within a respective one of the plurality of microspheres, each collected microsphere having a respective nucleic acid and an associated barcode; (d) re-emulsifying the collected microspheres; and (e) using the associated barcode to label the associated nucleic acid with a reaction. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein step (a) is performed under microfluidic control. 1 1. The method of claim 9, wherein step (a) is done with agitation or shaking. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein step (c) is performed under microfluidic control. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein step (c) is done with agitation or shaking. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein the polymeric support defines a polymeric solid support.
